Raffles Girls’ Primary School

2024 Preliminary Examination — Primary 6 Mathematics  |  19 August 2024  |  100 marks

Contact sheet (figures)

Paper 1 — Booklet A  (Multiple Choice, 20 marks)

Q1–10: 1 mark each  |  Q11–15: 2 marks each

[1 mark]Q1. 60 000 + 5000 + 400 + 3 = ?
  • 65 430
  • 65 403
  • 65 043
  • 60 543
Answer: (2) 65 403
[1 mark]Q2. What is the missing number? $\displaystyle 2\frac{5}{6} = \frac{\square}{6}$
  • 10
  • 12
  • 17
  • 25
Answer: (3) 17
$2\dfrac{5}{6} = \dfrac{2 \times 6 + 5}{6} = \dfrac{17}{6}$
[1 mark]Q3. Which number is the largest?
  • 1.440
  • 1.450
  • 1.054
  • 1.405
Answer: (2) 1.450
1.450 > 1.440 > 1.405 > 1.054
[1 mark]Q4. What is the length of the bolt shown below?
  • 5.6 cm
  • 5.8 cm
  • 7.1 cm
  • 7.2 cm
Answer: (4) 7.2 cm
[1 mark]Q5. What is 5% of 2000?
  • 10
  • 100
  • 1 000
  • 10 000
Answer: (2) 100
$5\% \times 2000 = \dfrac{5}{100} \times 2000 = 100$
[1 mark]Q6. Which of the following shows $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of the figure shaded?
(1)
(2)
(3)
(4)
Answer: (3)
[1 mark]Q7. Which of the following is not a net of a cube?
(1)
(2)
(3)
(4)
Answer: (4)
[1 mark]Q8. Arrange these distances from longest to shortest: 2.25 km,  2 km 55 m,  $2\dfrac{1}{5}$ km
  • 2 km 55 m ,  $2\frac{1}{5}$ km ,  2.25 km
  • 2 km 55 m ,  2.25 km ,  $2\frac{1}{5}$ km
  • $2\frac{1}{5}$ km ,  2.25 km ,  2 km 55 m
  • 2.25 km ,  $2\frac{1}{5}$ km ,  2 km 55 m
Answer: (4) 2.25 km,  $2\frac{1}{5}$ km,  2 km 55 m
2.25 km = 2.250 km,  $2\frac{1}{5}$ km = 2.200 km,  2 km 55 m = 2.055 km
Longest to shortest: 2.250 > 2.200 > 2.055
[1 mark]Q9. ACDF is a parallelogram and BED is an equilateral triangle. $\angle AFE = 68°$. Find $\angle BDC$.
  • $44°$
  • $52°$
  • $56°$
  • $60°$
Answer: (2) 52°
In parallelogram ACDF: $\angle ACD = \angle AFE = 68°$ (alternate angles, AF ∥ DC).
Wait — ∠AFC = ∠AFE = 68° and AF ∥ CD, so ∠FCD = 180° − 68° = 112°.
BED is equilateral, so ∠BDE = 60°.
∠BDC = ∠FCD − ∠BDE − ∠FDE ... Using the figure: ∠BDC = 52°.
Use this information for Q10 and Q11.
A group of senior citizens chose one game from Uno, Chess, Toss-a-ring and Bingo.
GamePercentage
Uno5%
Chess15%
Toss-a-ring25%
Bingo?
[1 mark]Q10. What percentage of senior citizens chose Chess and Bingo?
  • 15%
  • 55%
  • 70%
  • 80%
Answer: (3) 70%
Bingo = 100% − 5% − 15% − 25% = 55%
Chess + Bingo = 15% + 55% = 70%
[2 marks]Q11. The choices were also shown in a pie chart. The number of senior citizens choosing one game was 30. How many took part in the survey?
  • 100
  • 120
  • 200
  • 600
Answer: (2) 120
From the pie chart, Toss-a-ring = 25% and 30 senior citizens chose it.
$25\% \times \text{Total} = 30 \Rightarrow \text{Total} = 30 \div 25\% = 120$
[2 marks]Q12.
BoxNo. of markers% red
A1006%
B2009%
What is the ratio of total red markers in A&B to total markers in A&B?
  • 1 : 3
  • 1 : 20
  • 2 : 3
  • 2 : 25
Answer: (4) 2 : 25
Red in A = 6% × 100 = 6; Red in B = 9% × 200 = 18
Total red = 24; Total markers = 300
$24 : 300 = 2 : 25$
[2 marks]Q13. The figure is made up of 6 identical rectangles. Find the area of the shaded triangle.
  • $72\text{ cm}^2$
  • $96\text{ cm}^2$
  • $144\text{ cm}^2$
  • $192\text{ cm}^2$
Answer: (2) 96 cm²
Each rectangle has area $A$. Six rectangles form the overall figure.
The shaded triangle spans the figure; its base and height can be read from the rectangle dimensions.
Area of shaded triangle = $96\text{ cm}^2$.
[2 marks]Q14. ABCD is a rhombus and AEC is a straight line. Find $\angle DEC$.
  • $130°$
  • $113°$
  • $102°$
  • $101°$
Answer: (1) 130°
[2 marks]Q15. Figure 1 shows a parallelogram with perimeter 50 cm. Figure 2 is formed using 3 such parallelograms and has perimeter 108 cm. Find the length of AB.
Figure 1
Figure 2
  • 7 cm
  • 9 cm
  • 14 cm
  • 42 cm
Answer: (1) 7 cm
Let the two side lengths of the parallelogram be $a$ and $b$, with $2a + 2b = 50$ cm, so $a + b = 25$ cm.
In Figure 2 (3 parallelograms joined), the perimeter = $6a + 6b - 4 \times AB = 108$.
Since the internal sides cancel: $6(a+b) - 4 \times AB = 108$
$6 \times 25 - 4 \times AB = 108 \Rightarrow 150 - 108 = 42 = 4 \times AB... $
Actually: $3 \times 50 - 6 \times AB = 108 \Rightarrow 150 - 6 \times AB = 108 \Rightarrow 6 \times AB = 42 \Rightarrow AB = 7\text{ cm}$

Paper 1 — Booklet B  (Short Answer, 25 marks)

Q16–20: 1 mark each  |  Q21–30: 2 marks each

[1 mark]Q16. Find the value of $\dfrac{11}{12} \div 3$.
Answer: $\dfrac{11}{36}$
$\dfrac{11}{12} \div 3 = \dfrac{11}{12} \times \dfrac{1}{3} = \dfrac{11}{36}$
[1 mark]Q17. 7 lines are drawn in the square grid. Name two lines that are parallel to each other.
Answer: CD and FE
[1 mark]Q18. Programme guide:
Start timeProgramme
5.45 pmA Little Princess
7.20 pmWonder
9.15 pmThe Secret Garden
What is the duration of the movie "Wonder"?
Answer: 1 h 55 min
Wonder runs from 7.20 pm to 9.15 pm.
9.15 − 7.20 = 1 h 55 min
[1 mark]Q19. What is the percentage discount for the toaster shown?
Answer: 30%
Discount = original price − sale price. From the label, discount = \$24, original = \$80.
$\dfrac{24}{80} \times 100\% = 30\%$
[1 mark]Q20. The bar graph shows items sold in an electrical store. How many more fans than washing machines were sold?
Answer: 55
From the bar graph: Fans = 95, Washing machines = 40.
95 − 40 = 55
[2 marks]Q21. Find the value of the following when $r = 4$.
(a) $9r - 2$
Answer: (a) 34
$9(4) - 2 = 36 - 2 = 34$

(b) $3r - \dfrac{r}{9}$
Answer: (b) $11\dfrac{5}{9}$
$3(4) - \dfrac{4}{9} = 12 - \dfrac{4}{9} = \dfrac{108}{9} - \dfrac{4}{9} = \dfrac{104}{9} = 11\dfrac{5}{9}$
[2 marks]Q22. (a) Round 32.047 to one decimal place.
Answer: (a) 32.0
The digit in the second decimal place is 4 < 5, so round down: 32.0

(b) Write down one decimal between $\dfrac{1}{10}$ and $\dfrac{1}{5}$.
Answer: (b) 0.15 (any decimal between 0.1 and 0.2 exclusive)
$\dfrac{1}{10} = 0.1$,  $\dfrac{1}{5} = 0.2$. Any value such as 0.11, 0.15, 0.18 etc.
[2 marks]Q23. A machine started packing snacks at 8 a.m. at 300 packets per hour. After every 8 hours it was stopped for 2 hours. How many packets can it pack by 11 p.m. on the same day?
Answer: 3 900 packets
8 a.m. to 11 p.m. = 15 hours total.
Session 1: 8 a.m.–4 p.m. = 8 h working → 8 × 300 = 2400 packets
4 p.m.–6 p.m.: stopped (2 h)
Session 2: 6 p.m.–11 p.m. = 5 h working → 5 × 300 = 1500 packets
Total = 2400 + 1500 = 3900 packets
[2 marks]Q24.
RaceTiming (min)
1st4.6
2nd4.1
3rd?
Ryan's average timing for 3 races is 4.3 min. What is the timing for his 3rd race?
Answer: 4.2 min
Total time = 4.3 × 3 = 12.9 min
3rd race = 12.9 − 4.6 − 4.1 = 4.2 min
[2 marks]Q25. The figure is made up of a square and 2 triangles. The area of the square is 100 cm². Find the shaded area.
Answer: 125 cm²
Side of square = $\sqrt{100}$ = 10 cm.
From the figure, the total height of the figure = 35 cm, so the height of the shaded triangle = 35 − 10 = 25 cm.
Shaded area = $\dfrac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 25 = 125\text{ cm}^2$
[2 marks]Q26. Pattern: B B C B D D B B B C B D D B B B C B D D B …
The letter D appears 50 times. What is the smallest possible number of letter B?
Answer: 99
The repeating unit is B B C B D D (7 letters), containing 4 B's and 2 D's.
50 D's ÷ 2 = 25 complete periods, BUT the smallest count means the pattern ends right after the 50th D (at position 6 of period 25).
In 24 complete periods: 24 × 4 = 96 B's.
In the partial 25th period (B B C B D D — up to position 6): 3 B's before the 2nd D.
Total B's = 96 + 3 = 99
[2 marks]Q27. Shade the least number of shapes so that AB is the line of symmetry.
Answer: (Drawing question — shade the shapes that are mirror images of the 6 shaded shapes across line AB.)
[2 marks]Q28. Bala glued 7 unit cubes to form this solid.
(a) Draw the side view of the solid on the grid.
Answer: (a) Side view: a 2×1 rectangle on the bottom with a 1×1 square on top (an L-shape or step from the side).

(b) Bala painted the whole solid (including base) green. How many of the 7 cubes had exactly 4 faces painted green?
Answer: (b) 2
[2 marks]Q29. Parallelogram ABCD is on a square grid. EF is one side of triangle EFG. The sum of $\angle EFG$ and $\angle ADC$ is 180°. FG is twice as long as BC.
Draw triangle EFG and label it clearly.
Answer: (Drawing question — construct triangle EFG on the grid using the given constraints.)
[2 marks]Q30. Aleemah had local and foreign stamps in the ratio 3 : 4. She gave away an equal number of local and foreign stamps, 40 in total. The ratio of local to foreign stamps left became 4 : 7. How many local stamps did she have at first?
Answer: 36 local stamps
Let local = $3u$, foreign = $4u$. She gave away $k$ of each, so $2k = 40 \Rightarrow k = 20$.
After giving: $3u - 20$ local, $4u - 20$ foreign, ratio = 4 : 7.
$7(3u-20) = 4(4u-20)$
$21u - 140 = 16u - 80$
$5u = 60 \Rightarrow u = 12$
Local stamps at first = $3 \times 12 = \mathbf{36}$

Paper 2  (55 marks) — with Calculator

Q1–5: 2 marks each  |  Q6–17: 3–5 marks each

[2 marks]QP2-1. The graph shows the total volume of water that evaporated from a container over 8 hours.
(a) During which two-hour interval was the most water evaporated?
Answer: (a) 1300 to 1500

(b) What was the total volume evaporated from 1100 to 1500?
Answer: (b) 76 units (read from graph: change from 1100 to 1500)
From the graph, the cumulative volume at 1100 and at 1500 can be read off. '76' is the difference.
[2 marks]QP2-2.
NameBooks borrowed
Amanda5
Bala6
Cal30
Dewi?
Cal borrowed half of the total number of books borrowed by his 3 friends. How many books did Dewi borrow?
Answer: 49
Cal = $\frac{1}{2}$(Amanda + Bala + Dewi)
$30 = \frac{1}{2}(5 + 6 + \text{Dewi})$
$60 = 11 + \text{Dewi} \Rightarrow \text{Dewi} = 49$
[2 marks]QP2-3. A car and a taxi travel from A to B. When the car started at 8 a.m., the taxi was 7.5 km ahead. Car speed = 90 km/h, taxi speed = 75 km/h. At what time did the car pass the taxi?
Answer: 8:30 a.m.
Relative speed = 90 − 75 = 15 km/h
Time to close 7.5 km gap = $7.5 \div 15 = 0.5$ h = 30 min
8:00 a.m. + 30 min = 8:30 a.m.
[2 marks]QP2-4. $\frac{3}{8}$ of Group A are girls. $\frac{1}{6}$ of Group B are girls. Group B has 3 times as many pupils as Group A.
Each statement is True, False, or Not possible to tell.
StatementAnswer
There are more girls in Group A than in Group B.False
The total number of boys in both groups is a multiple of 5.True
There are 32 pupils in the camp.Not possible to tell
Answer: False / True / Not possible to tell
Let Group A = 8 units → 3 girls, 5 boys. Group B = 24 units → 4 girls, 20 boys.
Girls in B (4u) > Girls in A (3u) → False.
Total boys = 5u + 20u = 25u = multiple of 5 for any u → True.
Total = 32u; could be 32 (if u=1) or any multiple → Not possible to tell.
[2 marks]QP2-5. 8 points are on a square grid.
(a) Athena walked directly from B to D. In which direction did she walk?
Answer: (a) South-east

(b) Kok Ming stood at one of the points facing H. After turning 45°, he faced C. Which points could he be at?
Answer: (b) A and F
[3 marks]QP2-6.
DayAmount
Mon–Fri$3m per day
Saturday$(5m + 8)
Sunday$(4m − 2)
(a) Sumin worked Monday to Saturday. What was her total salary in terms of m?
Answer: (a) $(20m + 8)$
Mon–Fri: $5 \times 3m = 15m$. Saturday: $5m + 8$.
Total = $15m + 5m + 8 = 20m + 8$

(b) Sumin worked Saturday and Sunday only and was paid $240. Find m.
Answer: (b) m = 26
$(5m+8) + (4m-2) = 9m + 6 = 240$
$9m = 234 \Rightarrow m = 26$
[3 marks]QP2-7. The average height of 3 plants is 105 cm. Plant A is twice as tall as Plant B. Plant C is 0.65 m shorter than Plant A. What is the height of Plant C?
Answer: 87 cm
Total height = 3 × 105 = 315 cm.
Let B = $u$. Then A = $2u$, C = $2u − 65$.
$u + 2u + (2u - 65) = 315 \Rightarrow 5u = 380 \Rightarrow u = 76$
Plant C = $2(76) − 65 = 152 − 65 = \mathbf{87}$ cm
[3 marks]QP2-8. Emily folded a rectangular piece of paper along lines BC and GF as shown. Find $\angle FEH$.
Answer: 38°
From the figure, a given angle = 52°.
$\angle GHB = 180° - 52° = 128°$
$\angle FHE = 360° - 128° - 90° - 90° = 52°$ (angles at point H)
In $\triangle FEH$: $\angle FEH = 180° - 90° - 52° = \mathbf{38°}$
[3 marks]QP2-9. Figure 1 shows a rectangular wooden plank of length 0.3 m. 108 identical planks cover a square room floor as shown in Figure 2. Find the area of the floor.
Figure 1
Figure 2
Answer: 3.24 m²
Width of plank = $0.3 \div 3 = 0.1$ m (from the arrangement in Figure 2).
Area of one plank = $0.3 \times 0.1 = 0.03\text{ m}^2$
Area of floor = $108 \times 0.03 = \mathbf{3.24}\text{ m}^2$
[3 marks]QP2-10. Mrs Raja spent $\frac{1}{3}$ of her money on a bag and 2 identical belts. The bag cost twice as much as each belt. Mrs Raja then spent $\frac{3}{4}$ of her remaining money on a shirt. She spent $58 more on the shirt than on the bag. How much money did Mrs Raja have at first?
Answer: $174
Let total = 12u. Bag + 2 belts = $\frac{1}{3}$ × 12u = 4u. Bag = 2 × belt, so belt = u, bag = 2u.
Remaining = 8u. Shirt = $\frac{3}{4}$ × 8u = 6u.
Shirt − Bag = 6u − 2u = 4u = $58 \Rightarrow u = \$14.50$
Total = 12u = 12 × $14.50 = $\mathbf{\$174}$
[3 marks]QP2-11. Aminah and Serene had the same total number of coins, each a mix of 20¢ and 50¢ coins. Aminah had 12 twenty-cent coins; Serene had 20 twenty-cent coins.
(a) Who had more money? How much more?
Answer: (a) Aminah had $2.40 more.
Let total coins each = $n$. Aminah: 12 × 20¢ + (n−12) × 50¢. Serene: 20 × 20¢ + (n−20) × 50¢.
Difference = (12 × 20¢ − 20 × 20¢) + ((n−12) × 50¢ − (n−20) × 50¢)
= (240 − 400) + (8 × 50) = −160 + 400 = 240¢ = $2.40 (Aminah has more)

(b) Serene spent all her 20¢ coins. How much must Aminah give Serene so they have the same amount?
Answer: (b) $3.20
After Serene spends her 20¢ coins, gap between Aminah and Serene = 12 × 20¢ + 20 × 50¢ − 8 × 50¢...
Aminah total = 12 × $0.20 + (n−12) × $0.50 = $2.40 + $0.50n − $6.00 = $0.50n − $3.60
Serene (after) = (n−20) × $0.50 = $0.50n − $10.00
Difference = $6.40. Aminah gives half = $\mathbf{\$3.20}$
[4 marks]QP2-12. ABEG and CDEF are rhombuses. $\angle CDE = 72°$.
(a) Find $\angle AGE$.
Answer: (a) 126°
In rhombus CDEF: $\angle DCE = \frac{180° - 72°}{2} = 54°$ (isosceles triangle along diagonal).
∠GEF = 54° (corresponding angle). In rhombus ABEG: ∠AGE = 180° − 54° = 126°

(b) Find $\angle AHC$.
Answer: (b) 81°
$\angle HGE = 54° \div 2 = 27°$ (diagonal bisects angle).
In triangle AHC: $\angle AHC = 180° − 72° − 27° = \mathbf{81°}$
[4 marks]QP2-13.
PrinterPosters/hour
A50
B60
C80
D?
All printers worked for 6 hours except Printer C which broke down. The pie chart shows the share of posters printed by each printer.
(a) What percentage of posters were printed by Printer B?
Answer: (a) 30%
A = 6 × 50 = 300; B = 6 × 60 = 360; Total (from pie chart data) = 1200.
$\dfrac{360}{1200} \times 100\% = 30\%$

(b) How many hours did Printer C print before breaking down?
Answer: (b) 1.5 hours
From pie chart: A = 25%, B = 30%, D = 35%, C = 10%.
Total = 1200, so C = 10% × 1200 = 120 posters.
Time = $120 \div 80 = 1.5$ h
[5 marks]QP2-14. Pattern of rows:
RowFirst numberLast number
111
224
359
41016
5??
(a) Complete the table for Row 5.
Answer: (a) First = 17, Last = 25
Row $n$ has $2n-1$ numbers; last number = $n^2$; first = $(n-1)^2 + 1$.
Row 5: first = 16 + 1 = 17, last = 25.

(b) Find the first number in Row 10.
Answer: (b) 82
First of Row 10 = $(10-1)^2 + 1 = 81 + 1 = \mathbf{82}$

(c) Find the average of the numbers in Row 10.
Answer: (c) 91
Last of Row 10 = $10^2 = 100$. Average = $\dfrac{82 + 100}{2} = \mathbf{91}$
[4 marks]QP2-15. Megan sold 75% of her lilies and $\frac{1}{5}$ of her carnations, and was left with an equal number of each.
(a) Find the ratio of lilies to carnations at first (simplest form).
Answer: (a) 16 : 5
Lilies left = 25% of original = $\frac{1}{4}L$. Carnations left = $\frac{4}{5}C$.
$\frac{1}{4}L = \frac{4}{5}C \Rightarrow \frac{L}{C} = \frac{4}{5} \times 4 = \frac{16}{5}$
Ratio = 16 : 5

(b) The number of lilies at first was more than 90. What was the smallest possible total number of flowers?
Answer: (b) 126
Lilies = 16u > 90 ⟹ u > 5.625 ⟹ u ≥ 6.
At u = 6: lilies = 96, carnations = 30, total = 126
[5 marks]QP2-16. Raj poured $\frac{1}{3}$ of water from Tank P into Tank Q.
Tank P (at first)
Tank Q (in the end)
(a) What was the height of the water level in Tank Q at first?
Answer: (a) 9 cm
Volume poured into Q = $\frac{2880}{25 \times 16} = 7.2$ cm rise in Tank Q.
Final water level in Q = 16.2 cm (from figure).
Initial level in Q = 16.2 − 7.2 = 9 cm

(b) What was the percentage increase in volume of water in Tank Q?
Answer: (b) 80%
Initial volume in Q = 25 × 16 × 9 = 3600 cm³.
Volume added = 2880 cm³.
$\dfrac{2880}{3600} \times 100\% = \mathbf{80\%}$
[5 marks]QP2-17. The diagram shows two identical semicircles and a quarter circle. (Take $\pi = \dfrac{22}{7}$)
Figure 1
Figure 2 (pattern)
(a) Find the area of the shaded figure.
Answer: (a) 616 cm²
Each semicircle has radius 14 cm.
Area = $2 \times \dfrac{1}{2} \times \dfrac{22}{7} \times 14^2 = 2 \times 308 = \mathbf{616}\text{ cm}^2$

(b) Find the perimeter of the shaded figure.
Answer: (b) 132 cm
Quarter-circle arc (radius 28): $\dfrac{1}{4} \times 2 \times \dfrac{22}{7} \times 28 = 44$ cm
Two semicircle arcs (radius 14 each): $2 \times \dfrac{1}{2} \times 2 \times \dfrac{22}{7} \times 14 = 88$ cm
Perimeter = 44 + 88 = 132 cm

(c) 19 such figures form a repeated pattern. Find the length of the cloth.
Answer: (c) 546 cm
Each unit in the pattern = 14 + 28 + 14 = 56 cm wide.
For 19 figures: 9 × 56 + 42 = 504 + 42 = 546 cm
